revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chitose Kurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:千歳車塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Kameoka, Kyoto, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =千歳車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 080421 Chitose Kurumaduka Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.057028|N|135.570611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|en|Kameoka, Kyoto|qid=Q633996|lt=Kameoka, Kyoto|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early-6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|25.34|N|135|34|14.40|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun'''|千歳車塚古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Chitose neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|en|Kameoka, Kyoto|qid=Q633996|lt=Kameoka, Kyoto|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1982.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138967 |title=千歳車塚古墳|trans-title= Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built in the first half of the 6th century (the first half of the late Kofun period), and is the largest tumulus in the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba region|1=Tanba Province}}. Per local legend, it is the tomb of King Yahiko (the 14th grandson of [[:en:Emperor Chuai|Emperor Chuai]]). ==Overview== The Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun is located on a small hill extending from Mt. Inatsuki in the northeastern part of the Kameoka Basin, on the left bank of the Oi River. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated to the northwest and has a current overall length of 82 meters, but may have been up to 88 meters long. It is somewhat unusual in shape in that the width of the anterior part exceeds the diameter of the posterior circle. The left constriction of the mound may have a ceremonial platform. The tumulus was once covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and had rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. The tumulus was surrounded by two concentric moats, of which traces of the inner moat still remain. Another unusual feature of the tumulus is that the moats are asymmetrical with respect to the main axis of the Kofun, for unknown reasons. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details remain uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The design of the tumulus resembles that of the Imashirozuka Kofun in [[:en:Takatsuki, Osaka|Takatsuki, Osaka]], which is believed to be the true tomb of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, and to date from the same period, leading to the theory is that the person buried here was a candidate for succession to the throne of the same Rank as Emperor Keitai. Analysis of the clay used indicates that the ''haniwa'' were produced at the Shinike Hanawa Production Site in Takatsuki. This indicates a strong connection between the Yamato kingship and the person buried in this tumulus. The tumulus was designated as a Kameoka City Historic Site in 1970 and was elevated to a National Historic Site in 1982. In the surrounding area are the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of Tanba Province, [[Izumo-daijingū]] and the ruins of the {{ill|Tanba Kokubun-ji|en|Tanba Kokubun-ji|qid=Q10877355|lt=Tanba Kokubun-ji|1=Tanba Kokubun-ji}} and Tanba Kokubun-niji. ;Total length: 82 meters (original length: 88 meters) ;Anterior rectangular portion: 49 meters wide x 7 meters high, 3-tier (original width: 54 meters) ;Posterior circular portion: 46 meter diameter x 7.5 meters high, 3-tiers (original diameter: 54 meters) ==Gallery== <gallery> Chitose Kurumazuka Kofun zenkei.JPG|Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun Chitose Kurumazuka Kofun zenkei-3.jpg|Chitose-Kurumazuka Kofun after grass is burned off in spring Chitose Kurumazuka Kofun haniwa.jpg|Fragments of ''haniwa'' in situ. </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|qid=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{template-inline|Chitose Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} *[https://www.kameoka.info/seeing/kurumazuka.php Kameoka City Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Chitose Kurumazuka Kofun]]
